ETH.pod_override_reason: Active debt restructuring — liquidity risk set to maximum

fiscal.description: Fiscal deficit moderating but reconstruction spending creates off-budget risks. Revenue collection improving from conflict lows. Data quality limits ATLAS confidence — applies uncertainty discount.
fiscal.warning: Data quality flag: all fiscal indicators have ±30% uncertainty band

external.description: Acute external financing constraints following debt moratorium. G20 Common Framework restructuring ongoing with official creditors. Reserves critically low at ~1.4 months import cover. Parallel market premium at 35% signals rationing.
external.warning: Active restructuring — external access effectively closed to private markets

fx.description: Birr devaluation July 2024 (+30%) was a structural step, but managed float implementation is fragile. Parallel market re-emerging with 35% premium. REER shows extreme undervaluation at -42.5%, suggesting further adjustment pressure.
fx.warning: Illiquid market — no reliable implied volatility pricing available

growth.description: Growth recovering post-conflict but data reliability is severely limited. IMF estimates 5.5% but ATLAS applies a ±2pp uncertainty band. Agricultural sector showing peace dividend; services sector reconstruction-driven.

political.description: Post-Pretoria Peace Agreement stabilization progressing. Regional tensions persist in Amhara and Oromia. IMF ECF program provides institutional anchor. No active armed conflict in 2026 but political fragmentation risk remains elevated.

liquidity.description: No market access — Ethiopia is effectively locked out of international capital markets during restructuring. Reserves critically low. IMF ECF provides the only significant external financing anchor. PoD override forces maximum liquidity risk score.
liquidity.warning: PoD override active: Liquidity Risk forced to ≥95 during active restructuring

fiscal.description: Post-restructuring fiscal consolidation progressing under IMF ECF targets. Revenue mobilization improving but expenditure discipline remains the binding constraint. Domestic debt exchange losses absorbed by banking sector.

external.description: External debt restructuring completed in 2024. Current account deficit narrowing on improved gold and cocoa exports. Reserves rebuilding but still below pre-crisis levels.

fx.description: Cedi stabilizing after 2022 crisis but depreciation pressure persists. Parallel market premium at ~7% — notable but not critical. FX market thin with limited interbank activity.

growth.description: Growth recovering from 2022 crisis trough. IMF projects gradual improvement toward 4% by 2026. Mining and cocoa sectors providing the primary growth impulse.

political.description: Stable democratic governance with smooth power transitions. 2024 election conducted peacefully. Strong institutional credibility by regional standards. IMF program provides policy anchor.

liquidity.description: Limited market access post-restructuring — Eurobond issuance likely feasible by 2027. IMF ECF and bilateral support providing bridge financing. Restructuring overhang still affects investor confidence.

fiscal.description: Twin deficit persists with fiscal balance under pressure from debt service obligations. Revenue mobilization through Finance Act 2023 improved fiscal metrics but generated political backlash. High interest expenditure-to-revenue ratio.

external.description: Eurobond maturity wall 2024-2027 is the primary external risk. Current account deficit manageable at ~5% GDP. Reserves adequate at ~4 months import cover. Diaspora remittances provide steady FX inflow.

fx.description: Shilling depreciation managed by CBK with relatively tight parallel market premium. FX market more liquid than most SSA peers. REER near fair value suggesting no major misalignment.

growth.description: Diversified economy reduces single-commodity exposure. Services sector (tech, finance, tourism) providing structural growth support. Agriculture remains vulnerable to climate shocks.

political.description: Political stability moderate with 2027 election cycle approaching. Institutional quality above SSA average. Finance Act protests in 2023 demonstrated social pressure constraints on fiscal policy.

liquidity.description: Full market access maintained — Kenya successfully tapped Eurobond markets in 2024. IMF EFF program provides policy anchor. Debt sustainability adequate but sensitive to interest rate shocks.

fiscal.description: Fuel subsidy removal saves ~2% GDP annually, materially improving fiscal space. Revenue-to-GDP ratio remains among the lowest globally. Oil revenue volatility and production below OPEC quota constrain the upside.

external.description: Oil exports provide hard currency anchor but production below quota limits the benefit. Dangote refinery reducing fuel import bill structurally. Parallel market premium narrowing post-FX unification.

fx.description: Post-June 2023 FX unification narrowing the parallel market premium from >60% to ~8%. CBN reserves include swaps — net position weaker than headline. Market remains thin with limited price discovery.
fx.warning: CBN reserves include swaps — net figure lower than headline

growth.description: Large domestic market with demographic dividend. Non-oil growth improving in agriculture and services. Security challenges in the north affecting agricultural productivity in key regions.

political.description: Tinubu administration's reform momentum is the key variable. FX and subsidy reforms signal willingness to act but social pressure is building from inflation pass-through. Security challenges in the north persist.

liquidity.description: Full market access — Nigeria retains Eurobond issuance capacity. No IMF program (signaling self-sufficiency preference). Oil revenue provides base-case financing anchor.

fiscal.description: Prudent fiscal management with debt-to-GDP below WAEMU convergence criteria. Infrastructure investment rising but financed through concessional channels. CFA membership constrains fiscal-monetary coordination.

external.description: Cocoa export concentration creates commodity risk. CFA peg provides FX stability but limits external adjustment flexibility. Current account manageable.

fx.description: CFA franc hard peg to EUR eliminates FX volatility risk but also removes the exchange rate as an adjustment mechanism. No parallel market. REER near equilibrium.

growth.description: Strongest WAEMU performer with 6%+ GDP growth. Infrastructure investment and Abidjan urbanization driving services growth. Cocoa sector climate vulnerability is the primary downside risk.

political.description: Post-2020 election normalization progressing. Political succession risk as post-Ouattara transition approaches. Institutional quality above West African average.

liquidity.description: Full market access with successful Eurobond issuance history. WAEMU regional bond market provides additional financing channel. No restructuring overhang.

fiscal.description: Fiscal data revisions raised credibility concerns. Debt levels elevated from infrastructure spending. Hydrocarbon revenue expected to improve fiscal trajectory from 2026.
fiscal.warning: Fiscal data revisions in 2024 — credibility rebuilding

external.description: Sangomar oil and GTA gas coming online transform the external balance outlook. Remittances remain critical. Current account deficit expected to narrow materially.

fx.description: CFA franc peg provides stability. No parallel market activity. REER near equilibrium. Hydrocarbon inflows may create appreciation pressure medium-term.

growth.description: Young demographic with urbanization momentum. Hydrocarbon sector providing structural growth uplift. Traditional sectors (fishing, agriculture, remittances) remain important.

political.description: New political leadership following 2024 election signaling policy continuity. Anti-corruption agenda may create short-term uncertainty. Institutional quality adequate.

liquidity.description: Full market access via Eurobond and WAEMU regional markets. No restructuring risk. Hydrocarbon revenue expected to improve debt sustainability metrics.

fiscal.description: Prudent fiscal management with strong governance metrics. Limited fiscal space for countercyclical policy due to small economy. Concessional financing access is a strength.

external.description: Small open economy vulnerable to external shocks. Mining exports provide some hard currency. EAC trade integration deepening.

fx.description: BNR manages RWF against USD with minimal parallel market activity. FX market illiquid but stable. REER near equilibrium.

growth.description: Technology-led growth strategy diversifying from agriculture. Kigali Innovation City and conference tourism providing new growth vectors. Strong governance supports investment climate.

political.description: Strong governance and business environment rankings. Concentration of political power provides policy consistency but raises long-term transition risk.

liquidity.description: Limited market access — no Eurobond history. Concessional and bilateral lending are primary financing channels. Small economy limits private market appetite.

fiscal.description: SOE contingent liabilities (Eskom, Transnet) are the primary fiscal risk. Revenue administration strong (SARS). Fiscal consolidation constrained by political coalition dynamics.

external.description: Deep and liquid capital markets provide structural financing advantage. Current account near balance. AGOA trade preferences provide export market access.

fx.description: Rand floats freely with deep FX market liquidity. No parallel market. Implied volatility market exists and functions. REER moderately undervalued.

growth.description: Load-shedding energy crisis constraining GDP growth below potential. Renewable energy transition creating investment wave. High unemployment (>30%) limiting demand-side recovery.

political.description: GNU coalition providing policy stability signal after 2024 election. Institutional quality strong by EM standards. Political fragmentation risk moderate but manageable.

liquidity.description: Full market access with deep domestic bond market and regular international issuance. No restructuring risk. SARB credibility strong.

fiscal.description: Earthquake reconstruction adds temporary fiscal pressure but within manageable bounds. Strong institutional capacity for fiscal management. Dirham basket peg constrains fiscal-monetary coordination.

external.description: Diversified export base (automotive, phosphates, agriculture, tourism). EU proximity provides structural trade advantage. Current account manageable.

fx.description: Dirham managed within ±5% band against EUR/USD basket. No parallel market. FX market moderately liquid. REER near equilibrium, slightly overvalued.

growth.description: Manufacturing hub for European nearshoring (automotive, aerospace). 2030 FIFA World Cup co-hosting driving infrastructure investment. Water stress and drought are structural agricultural risks.

political.description: Stable monarchy with gradual institutional reform. Strong governance metrics by MENA/Africa standards. Social reform agenda progressing.

liquidity.description: Full market access with investment-grade adjacent credit quality. Deep domestic bond market. Eurobond issuance regular and well-received.

fiscal.description: Mega-project spending straining fiscal position. Interest expenditure-to-revenue ratio elevated. Ras El-Hekma deal provides one-off fiscal relief but structural deficit persists.

external.description: Suez Canal revenue disrupted by Red Sea rerouting. Tourism recovering but geopolitically sensitive. Gulf FDI (UAE, Saudi) providing financing anchor. Gas exports plateauing.

fx.description: Post-March 2024 devaluation (~60%) narrowed parallel market gap significantly. EGP managed float with CBE intervention. Market remains thin with limited price discovery.

growth.description: Large domestic market with young demographic. Tourism and Suez Canal traditionally key growth drivers. Inflation pass-through from devaluation constraining real income growth.

political.description: Centralized governance provides policy consistency. IMF EFF program conditions testing reform commitment on privatization and competition. Regional geopolitical exposure elevated.

liquidity.description: Full market access maintained through crisis. Gulf bilateral support + IMF EFF + Ras El-Hekma deal provide multi-layered financing. Eurobond market accessible at elevated spreads.
